evals.prompt:
Here is a CSV with monthly sales data for 3 product lines over 2 years. Analyze it: find trends, identify which product line is growing fastest, check for seasonality, and flag any anomalies. Give me the key findings.

Product,Month,Revenue
Widget-A,2024-01,45200
Widget-A,2024-02,43800
Widget-A,2024-03,51200
Widget-B,2024-01,12400
Widget-B,2024-02,18600
Widget-B,2024-03,22100
Widget-C,2024-01,98000
Widget-C,2024-02,95500
Widget-C,2024-03,88200
evals.expected_output: EDA covering trend direction per product line, growth rate comparison, note on Widget-C declining while Widget-B accelerates, check for seasonality pattern, summary statistics, and at least one anomaly flag or clean bill of health with reasoning.

evals.prompt: I have employee survey data. Group A (new process, n=45) had satisfaction scores: mean=7.2, std=1.4. Group B (old process, n=48) had scores: mean=6.5, std=1.6. Test whether the new process actually improved satisfaction or if this could be random chance.
evals.expected_output: Formulates H0/H1, selects independent-samples t-test, checks assumptions (normality, equal variance), computes or estimates t-statistic and p-value, calculates effect size (Cohen's d), interprets both statistical and practical significance, states conclusion in plain language.

evals.prompt:
Compare these three customer segments by revenue and churn rate. Tell me which segment to focus on and why.

Segment | Avg Revenue | Churn Rate | Count
Enterprise | $42,000 | 4% | 120
Mid-Market | $8,500 | 11% | 580
SMB | $1,200 | 28% | 2400
evals.expected_output: Calculates total revenue contribution per segment, lifetime value estimate factoring churn, identifies the highest-leverage segment, provides a driver analysis, and recommends a specific focus with quantified expected impact — not just 'it depends'.
